item.title: Hotfix 1.6.0.475 is live!
item.description:
Lord Captains!

Yet another hotfix 1.6.0.475 is on the way, fixing two major bugs:

  • Inability to jump into the vent during the Void Shadows storyline - fixed;
  • It was possible to gain access to Act V systems via Trazyn's Museion, completely breaking the game - fixed;

We are delivering this hotfix quickly to stop the Act V problem from appearing again, but reverting the progress on existing saves requires more research and will come in other upcoming updates. If you had this issue in your playthrough, for now please load an earlier save.

We are sorry for the inconvenience.

As usual, consoles will get the update with up to 48 hours delay.
item.content:encoded:
Lord Captains!

Yet another hotfix 1.6.0.475 is on the way, fixing two major bugs:

  • Inability to jump into the vent during the Void Shadows storyline - fixed;
  • It was possible to gain access to Act V systems via Trazyn's Museion, completely breaking the game - fixed;

We are delivering this hotfix quickly to stop the Act V problem from appearing again, but reverting the progress on existing saves requires more research and will come in other upcoming updates. If you had this issue in your playthrough, for now please load an earlier save.

We are sorry for the inconvenience.

As usual, consoles will get the update with up to 48 hours delay.

item.title: Connection / desync issues - Looking into it
item.description:
OI GITS!

We've seen a bunch more players jump on now that the EGS version was released yesterday - which is great

What's not-so-great is that unfortunately we've also seen reports of connection issues, with host/client desync and matches ending suddenly, kicking everyone back to the menu, as well as other things

We're looking into this as top priority and hope to have some fixes ASAP

In the meantime, here's some potential workarounds Can't join a game:

  • Try a different one for a bit then try the previous one again if you still want to join it

Can't join any games / can't create a game:

  • Restart Steam. Make sure Speed Freeks isn't running still in Task Manager

  • If that fails, restart your PC

Crashed or kicked back to menu while hosting, or general bad performance while hosting

  • Make sure there's no heavy processes running in the background

  • If you have medium to low end PC, you perhaps shouldn't be hosting as it requires more processing power than joining a hosted game

If any of these don't work, we'll have to wait for the patch then

We're sorry for the connection problems and appreciate your patience while we try to fix them

Hopefully you'z can still get sum krumpin' dun in da meantime though

We'll update you as soon as we have progress
item.content:encoded:
OI GITS!

We've seen a bunch more players jump on now that the EGS version was released yesterday - which is great

What's not-so-great is that unfortunately we've also seen reports of connection issues, with host/client desync and matches ending suddenly, kicking everyone back to the menu, as well as other things

We're looking into this as top priority and hope to have some fixes ASAP

In the meantime, here's some potential workarounds Can't join a game:

  • Try a different one for a bit then try the previous one again if you still want to join it

Can't join any games / can't create a game:

  • Restart Steam. Make sure Speed Freeks isn't running still in Task Manager

  • If that fails, restart your PC

Crashed or kicked back to menu while hosting, or general bad performance while hosting

  • Make sure there's no heavy processes running in the background

  • If you have medium to low end PC, you perhaps shouldn't be hosting as it requires more processing power than joining a hosted game

If any of these don't work, we'll have to wait for the patch then

We're sorry for the connection problems and appreciate your patience while we try to fix them

Hopefully you'z can still get sum krumpin' dun in da meantime though

We'll update you as soon as we have progress

item.title: Hotfix 1.6.0.474 is live!
item.description:
Lord Captains! We're deploying another hotfix 1.6.0.474 today, with the following fixes:

  • Removed odd pauses in multiple cutscenes;
  • Fixed duplicated buttons on hide/show UI on console;
  • The first combat on The Heartless and on Janus will no longer become stuck;
  • The ability icons will no longer sometimes gray out after cutscenes;
  • Some polish font characters were displayed incorrectly in the game - fixed;
  • Jocasta will no longer float in the air after the romance scene;
  • When bathing, the female Lord Captain will no longer be completely underwater;
  • Vladaym will no longer levitate in one of his dialogues;

The same update for GOG will arrive in a few hours. Consoles will receive it with a delay of 24-48 hours.
item.content:encoded:
Lord Captains! We're deploying another hotfix 1.6.0.474 today, with the following fixes:

  • Removed odd pauses in multiple cutscenes;
  • Fixed duplicated buttons on hide/show UI on console;
  • The first combat on The Heartless and on Janus will no longer become stuck;
  • The ability icons will no longer sometimes gray out after cutscenes;
  • Some polish font characters were displayed incorrectly in the game - fixed;
  • Jocasta will no longer float in the air after the romance scene;
  • When bathing, the female Lord Captain will no longer be completely underwater;
  • Vladaym will no longer levitate in one of his dialogues;

The same update for GOG will arrive in a few hours. Consoles will receive it with a delay of 24-48 hours.

item.title: Performance Update Available on Beta Branch
item.description:
Hello everyone.

As previously mentioned, the team is currently working on a performance-focused update for Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus II. This build includes a major engine update, with the aim of delivering meaningful performance and optimisation improvements.

The build is still undergoing testing on our side. However, because PC performance can vary significantly depending on hardware, drivers, settings, Steam cache state and other system-specific factors, we also want to gather feedback from a wider range of real player setups before moving the update to the main live branch.

For that reason, we’re making the upcoming performance update available through an optional public Steam beta branch.

This will allow players who are currently experiencing performance issues, or who simply want to help test the build, to try it ahead of the full release and share feedback with us.

Beta Branch Details

  • Branch name: public_beta

  • Branch password: mechanicus2beta

Instructions on how to access the branch:

  • Right-click Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus II in your Steam Library.

  • Select Properties.

  • Go to Betas.

  • Enter the beta access code above.

  • Select the beta branch from the dropdown once it appears.

  • Allow Steam to update the game.

To return to the current live version, go back to the Betas menu and select None.

Please remember:

This is an optional beta branch, not the default live version of the game.

The build is still in testing and may contain issues or regressions.

We’re not asking all players to switch over – only those who are comfortable testing a work-in-progress build.

The current live branch will remain available as normal.

Feedback from the beta will help us better understand performance across a wider range of hardware before the update is released fully.

Reporting Performance Issues

If on the beta branch you continue to experience severe optimisation or performance issues, please report them through our existing bug report form https://(https://forms.gle/L6JrusUkoSzaZU5PA).

When reporting performance issues, please include as much detail as possible, especially:

  • your hardware specifications

  • a DxDiag file, where possible

  • your in-game graphics settings

  • the mission/skirmish where the issue occurred

  • whether performance changed compared to the current live build

  • whether you had previously cleared your Steam shader/cache files on your local machine

Thank you again for your patience and for continuing to share detailed reports with us. We know the current performance issues have been frustrating, and we want to make sure this update is tested as thoroughly as possible before it becomes the main live build.
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone.

As previously mentioned, the team is currently working on a performance-focused update for Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus II. This build includes a major engine update, with the aim of delivering meaningful performance and optimisation improvements.

The build is still undergoing testing on our side. However, because PC performance can vary significantly depending on hardware, drivers, settings, Steam cache state and other system-specific factors, we also want to gather feedback from a wider range of real player setups before moving the update to the main live branch.

For that reason, we’re making the upcoming performance update available through an optional public Steam beta branch.

This will allow players who are currently experiencing performance issues, or who simply want to help test the build, to try it ahead of the full release and share feedback with us.

Beta Branch Details

  • Branch name: public_beta

  • Branch password: mechanicus2beta

Instructions on how to access the branch:

  • Right-click Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus II in your Steam Library.

  • Select Properties.

  • Go to Betas.

  • Enter the beta access code above.

  • Select the beta branch from the dropdown once it appears.

  • Allow Steam to update the game.

To return to the current live version, go back to the Betas menu and select None.

Please remember:

This is an optional beta branch, not the default live version of the game.

The build is still in testing and may contain issues or regressions.

We’re not asking all players to switch over – only those who are comfortable testing a work-in-progress build.

The current live branch will remain available as normal.

Feedback from the beta will help us better understand performance across a wider range of hardware before the update is released fully.

Reporting Performance Issues

If on the beta branch you continue to experience severe optimisation or performance issues, please report them through our existing bug report form https://(https://forms.gle/L6JrusUkoSzaZU5PA).

When reporting performance issues, please include as much detail as possible, especially:

  • your hardware specifications

  • a DxDiag file, where possible

  • your in-game graphics settings

  • the mission/skirmish where the issue occurred

  • whether performance changed compared to the current live build

  • whether you had previously cleared your Steam shader/cache files on your local machine

Thank you again for your patience and for continuing to share detailed reports with us. We know the current performance issues have been frustrating, and we want to make sure this update is tested as thoroughly as possible before it becomes the main live build.

item.title: Hotfix 1.6.0.472 is live!
item.description:
Following the release of the Infinite Museion DLC, we're quickly deploying a hotfix that will address a few issues, including the loading scene crash that a number of players seem to experience.

Patch Notes:

  • Fixed a crash during loading screens that sent you back to the main menu;
  • Fixed an issue where the timer in one of the locations remained visible on the screen during cutscenes. It is now hidden while a cutscene is playing and restored once it ends;
  • Fixed a localization display issue affecting Chinese text in the dialogue with Ferrenz;
  • Multi-Melta names were arranged incorrectly - fixed;
  • Fixed an issue where Calcazar could remain standing at the cogitator for a long time after the cutscene had finished;
  • Fixed Error speaker in dialogue at Zarkov;
  • Fixed Error speakers in dialogue at the Museion;
  • Fixed incorrect Vedka portrait being shown under some circumstances;
item.content:encoded:
Following the release of the Infinite Museion DLC, we're quickly deploying a hotfix that will address a few issues, including the loading scene crash that a number of players seem to experience.

Patch Notes:

  • Fixed a crash during loading screens that sent you back to the main menu;
  • Fixed an issue where the timer in one of the locations remained visible on the screen during cutscenes. It is now hidden while a cutscene is playing and restored once it ends;
  • Fixed a localization display issue affecting Chinese text in the dialogue with Ferrenz;
  • Multi-Melta names were arranged incorrectly - fixed;
  • Fixed an issue where Calcazar could remain standing at the cogitator for a long time after the cutscene had finished;
  • Fixed Error speaker in dialogue at Zarkov;
  • Fixed Error speakers in dialogue at the Museion;
  • Fixed incorrect Vedka portrait being shown under some circumstances;
